summary: Task 03 cleanly completes the report-side pipeline for procpidstat. Six files modified: NotRecordable removed from the procpidstat view block; metadata struct extended with ticks/cpuCount; isFilenameOK accepts sysinfo; readTar gains a sysinfo decode branch that merges into the running metadata without altering the metaOK/statOK gate; describeReport maps procpidstat to a new package-level constant; processData gains one-shot WARNING detection (via extracted helper emitProcPidStatAvailabilityWarnings with named column constants) and a no-data INFO path keyed on ReportType==procpidstat. CLI flag wired through cmd/report/report.go + cmd/help.go. Build clean, go vet clean, gofmt clean on changed files, 16/16 report tests green (3 new TDD tests + 1 added WARNING test with 6 sub-cases). Pre-existing gofmt drift in internal/view/view.go (unrelated to changes) was correctly left untouched per global rules.

suggestions.suggestion: The sysinfo branch in readTar correctly preserves any prior sysinfo merge when meta is read after sysinfo (m.ticks = meta.ticks; m.cpuCount = meta.cpuCount before reassigning meta = m). This pattern is non-obvious and could be lost in a future refactor. The inline comment explains it, but consider an integration test that arranges entries in the order sysinfo→meta→stat to pin the carry-over semantics. Optional — the current recorder writes sysinfo after stats, so this code path is defensive against future ordering changes only.
suggestions.benefit: Lock in the carry-over behavior under explicit test coverage; protects against regressions if a future reader sorts/reorders tar entries.

suggestions.suggestion: Column index constants (procPidStatColReadTotalKiB=9, procPidStatColWriteTotalKiB=10, procPidStatColIODelayTotalS=11) duplicate magic numbers already implicit in the 19-column procpidstat layout defined in internal/stat/procpidstat.go. Consider exporting the column-index constants from internal/stat alongside the column-name constants in a follow-up, so the report package imports them rather than redeclaring. Not in scope for Task 03; the local declaration with a doc-comment reference back to internal/stat is acceptable.
suggestions.benefit: Single source of truth for the procpidstat column layout; future column shifts only require one edit in stat package.

suggestions.suggestion: Test_readMeta_with_sysinfo asserts on items[1] (second tick) because the recorder writes sysinfo AFTER stats, so the first tick's data send happens before sysinfo is read. The test comment documents this design clearly. The task description text reads as if a single-tick fixture should suffice — the deviation is explicitly noted in decisions.md. Consider also adding a single-entry assertion that meta.ticks=0 in items[0] to make the one-tick-lag contract explicit.
suggestions.benefit: Documents the one-tick-delayed sysinfo merge as a tested contract rather than an implementation accident.

metrics.recordPackageFailures: expected — Test_filterViews, TestFilterViews_NotRecordable, Test_app_record are explicitly Task 04 scope per tech-spec Wave 3
